Title: AtMYBS1 negatively regulates heat tolerance by directly repressing the expression of MAX1 required for strigolactone biosynthesis in Arabidopsis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of carlactone to downstream strigolactone intermediates/noncanonical SL branch via carlactonoic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by heat stress in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated during heat treatment; reduced in AtMYBS1-OE and increased in atmybs1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes carlactone to carlactonoic acid in strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone-mediated heat tolerance; loss of function causes heat hypersensitivity and increased branching, while overexpression enhances heat tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAX1 expression is induced by heat stress and inversely correlated with AtMYBS1 expression. 2. max1 mutants are heat sensitive, whereas 35S:MAX1 plants are heat tolerant. 3. Overexpression of MAX1 rescues the heat hypersensitivity and branching defects of AtMYBS1-OE plants, while max1 abolishes the heat-tolerant phenotype of atmybs1 mutants. 4. Mutation of the MYB-binding site in the MAX1 promoter increases MAX1 expression and heat tolerance, demonstrating direct repression by AtMYBS1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant; overexpression; genetic complementation/rescue; double-mutant analysis; promoter mutation transgenics; qRT-PCR; luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; GR24^4DO application
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone function in shoot architecture and positive regulation of heat tolerance/stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upregulating MAX1 or disrupting its repression by AtMYBS1 at the MYB-binding site could improve thermotolerance, but potential shoot architecture effects such as altered branching should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of MAX1 causes hyperbranching and heat sensitivity; altered expression affects plant architecture
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes that MAX1 is essential for both canonical and noncanonical SL biosynthesis and that Arabidopsis SL measurements were technically difficult; some effects could theoretically involve substrate accumulation, but genetic and GR24 evidence support SL-mediated action
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive; native MAX1 promoter; mutated MAX1 promoter with altered MYB-binding site (AACTAAC to AACTCCG)

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Slightly decreased by heat stress; increased in AtMYBS1-OE and slightly decreased in atmybs1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Slightly decreased under heat stress; elevated in AtMYBS1-OE due to feedback
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Stereospecifically cleaves 9-cis-beta-carotene to produce 9-cis-beta-apo-10'-carotenal and beta-ionone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes heat tolerance through SL biosynthesis; max3 mutants are heat sensitive and 35S:MAX3 plants are heat tolerant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. max3 mutants are hypersensitive to heat stress. 2. Overexpression of MAX3 confers heat tolerance. 3. Overexpressing MAX1 cannot rescue max3 heat sensitivity, indicating MAX3-derived products are required upstream of MAX1. 4. GR24^4DO rescues heat sensitivity of max3-related genotypes, supporting SL pathway involvement.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant; overexpression; epistasis with 35S:MAX1/max3; GR24^4DO application; heat survival assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone function in shoot architecture and positive regulation of heat tolerance/stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MAX3 upregulation may be useful to enhance heat tolerance through SL biosynthesis, likely in combination with downstream intact pathway components
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential architecture effects linked to altered SL biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression changes under heat appear smaller than for MAX1 and may reflect negative feedback from altered SL status

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Slightly decreased by heat stress; increased in AtMYBS1-OE and slightly decreased in atmybs1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Slightly decreased under heat stress; elevated in AtMYBS1-OE due to feedback
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes heat tolerance through SL biosynthesis; max4 mutants are heat sensitive and 35S:MAX4 plants are heat tolerant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. max4 mutants are hypersensitive to heat stress. 2. Overexpression of MAX4 confers heat tolerance. 3. Overexpressing MAX1 cannot rescue max4 heat sensitivity, indicating MAX4-derived precursor production is required upstream of MAX1. 4. GR24^4DO rescues heat sensitivity of max4-related genotypes, supporting SL pathway involvement.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant; overexpression; epistasis with 35S:MAX1/max4; GR24^4DO application; heat survival assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone function in shoot architecture and positive regulation of heat tolerance/stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MAX4 upregulation may support engineering of heat tolerance if downstream SL signaling remains functional
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential architecture effects linked to altered SL biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression changes under heat are modest and likely influenced by SL feedback regulation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone function in shoot architecture and positive regulation of heat tolerance/stress resistance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Downregulated by heat stress in seedlings; promoter-GUS also decreases under heat
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced during heat treatment; overexpression causes heat hypersensitivity and increased branching
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses MAX1 expression by binding the MYB-binding site in the MAX1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative upstream controller of strigolactone biosynthesis-mediated heat tolerance through direct repression of MAX1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces strigolactone biosynthesis/signaling output and negatively regulates heat tolerance; overexpression causes heat hypersensitivity whereas loss of function enhances heat tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYBS1 is strongly downregulated by heat stress. 2. AtMYBS1 overexpression causes heat hypersensitivity, whereas atmybs1 mutants show increased heat tolerance. 3. AtMYBS1 directly binds the MYB-binding site in the MAX1 promoter and represses MAX1 transcription. 4. Genetic rescue and double-mutant analyses show that AtMYBS1 regulates heat tolerance through MAX1 and requires SL signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; T-DNA knockout mutants; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS assay; yeast one-hybrid; ChIP-qPCR; transient luciferase assay; genetic rescue with 35S:MAX1; double mutants with max1 and d14; promoter-site mutation transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knocking down AtMYBS1 or editing its MAX1-promoter binding module could enhance heat tolerance via increased SL biosynthesis, but pleiotropic effects on architecture, senescence, ABA/sugar responses, and stress tradeoffs should be monitored
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes increased branching, reduced height, rounder lighter green leaves, and heat hypersensitivity; AtMYBS1 is also implicated in ABA/sugar/salt response pathways
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also known as AtMYBL; the study demonstrates in vivo dependence on the MYB-binding site AACTAAC in the MAX1 promoter; AtMYBS1-regulated heat tolerance requires D14-mediated SL signaling

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone signaling for shoot architecture and heat tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Signal perception required for downstream response
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for SL-dependent activation of heat-tolerance responses downstream of biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential signaling component required for AtMYBS1-MAX1-mediated heat tolerance output
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with MAX2 in SL signaling according to background described in paper
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive requirement for strigolactone-mediated heat tolerance; d14 mutants are heat hypersensitive and cannot be rescued by GR24^4DO
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. d14 mutants are hypersensitive to heat stress. 2. GR24^4DO does not rescue d14 heat sensitivity, showing D14 is required for SL signaling output. 3. Loss of D14 abolishes the heat-tolerant effect of 35S:MAX1. 4. atmybs1d14 double mutants resemble d14, indicating AtMYBS1-regulated heat tolerance requires D14.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant; epistasis with 35S:MAX1d14 and atmybs1d14; GR24^4DO application; heat survival assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining intact D14 signaling is necessary when engineering upstream SL biosynthesis genes for thermotolerance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Defects in D14 block beneficial effects of elevated SL biosynthesis on heat tolerance and are associated with altered shoot architecture in SL-insensitive backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because the Results section functionally tests its necessity in the AtMYBS1-MAX1 heat-tolerance module
